content: Integrated Vector Management (IVM) combines multiple approaches: (1) Source reduction—eliminate standing water, improve waste management; (2) Larval control—apply larvicides to unavoidable breeding sites, use biological agents; (3) Adult control—space spraying during outbreaks, indoor residual spraying for endophilic vectors; (4) Personal protection—distribute insecticide-treated bed nets, promote repellent use; (5) Housing improvements—screens, air conditioning; (6) Community engagement—mobilize residents for source reduction. Insecticide resistance management: rotate insecticide classes, use combinations, preserve susceptible alleles through refugia. Surveillance monitors vector density, species composition, infection rates, and insecticide susceptibility.
source: WHO IVM guidelines, Pesticide Resistance Action Committee recommendations
